latex.title: A Survey of Modern Document Generation Systems
latex.author: AutoOffice Team
latex.abstract: This paper surveys the landscape of automated document generation systems, focusing on template-based approaches, AI-assisted content creation, and multi-format output pipelines. We analyze the trade-offs between flexibility, quality, and performance across different architectural patterns.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.body: Automated document generation has become a critical capability for modern software systems. Organizations increasingly need to produce reports, presentations, and technical documents from structured data sources with minimal manual intervention.

sections.heading: Template-Based Approaches
sections.body: Template engines such as Handlebars, Jinja2, and Mustache provide a declarative way to map data to document structure. These systems excel at producing consistent output but require upfront template design effort.

sections.heading: Evaluation Metrics
sections.body: We evaluate generation systems along three axes: output fidelity (visual quality compared to manually crafted documents), latency (time from input to final output), and maintainability (effort required to add new formats or modify templates).

bibliography: Knuth, D. E. (1984). The TeXbook. Addison-Wesley.
Lamport, L. (1994). LaTeX: A Document Preparation System. Addison-Wesley.
